title: The 'Doctor Who' TV movie at 30: Too British for America, too American for the UK
summary: A retrospective on the 30th anniversary of the 'Doctor Who' TV movie, highlighting its mixed reception in both the UK and US. The film's transatlantic production efforts to revive the franchise faced challenges in cultural adaptation.

title: No, this isn't a solar eclipse. It's an image of Mars, captured by NASA's asteroid-bound Psyche probe
summary: NASA's Psyche probe captured an image of Mars during its mission. The spacecraft is en route to the asteroid Psyche and performed a flyby of Mars on May 15.

title: 'Directive 8020' is an introspective look at sci-fi horror and our fear of the unknown
summary: 'Directive 8020' is a sci-fi horror game by Supermassive Games that explores humanity's fear of the unknown in space exploration. The game reflects on the uncertainties and existential dread tied to venturing into the cosmos.

title: NASA's Perseverance rover is positively glowing in its new selfie on Mars
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a new selfie on Mars, showcasing the rugged terrain of Jezero Crater and ancient geological features. The image highlights clues about the Red Planet's distant past.

title: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket will do something completely new on Flight 12 — take a good look at itself
summary: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket is scheduled for its first flight next week, featuring unprecedented in-space actions. This marks a significant advancement in the rocket's capabilities.

title: What should you do if you meet an alien? Neil deGrasse Tyson offers a scientific perspective in 'Take Me To Your Leader' (interview)
summary: Neil deGrasse Tyson discusses a scientific approach to potential alien encounters in an interview titled 'Take Me To Your Leader'. He advocates for a rational perspective amidst public fascination with extraterrestrial life.

title: Earth photobombs a satellite deploying its giant reflector | Space photo of the day for May 14, 2026
summary: ViaSat's ViaSat-3 F2 satellite deployed its giant reflector, which was captured in a space photo where Earth photobombed the view. This marks a significant milestone in satellite technology for ViaSat.

title: The world's biggest rocket: How SpaceX's new Starship 'V3' differs from its predecessors
summary: SpaceX's next-generation Starship 'V3' represents a complete overhaul of its predecessors, bringing the company closer to achieving human lunar missions. The new design marks a significant advancement in rocket technology.

title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
summary: NASA issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) for the Mars Telecommunications Network, seeking industry collaboration to enable high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will support surface, orbital, and human exploration by using high-performance Mars telecommunications orbiters.

title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
summary: NASA is partnering with Eta Space to conduct an in-orbit demonstration of cryogenic fuel technology, aiming to enable future deep space missions. The LOXSAT mission will test technologies for managing liquid oxygen in space, crucial for establishing in-space propellant depots.

title: Fresh Food Delivery for Space Station
summary: Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft delivered fresh food to the International Space Station, including oranges, apples, and onions. NASA and ESA astronauts celebrated the delivery with a fun photo in microgravity.

title: Ice Moves Out of Aniak
summary: Ice melt along Alaska's Kuskokwim River has led to ice jams and subsequent flooding. This natural event highlights the impacts of seasonal changes in the region.

title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
summary: NASA-supported 3D printing technology developed for lunar habitats is being adapted by Branch Technology Inc. to create efficient and strong building structures on Earth using lightweight lattice designs.

title: Rise Goes to Washington
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew, including astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, met with congressional staff in Washington on May 12, 2026, showcasing the mission's zero gravity indicator 'Rise.' The nearly 10-day Artemis II mission is a key step toward future lunar exploration.

title: NASA Outlines Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plans
summary: NASA is advancing plans for the Artemis III mission, a crewed flight in Earth orbit to test rendezvous and docking with commercial lunar landers from Blue Origin and SpaceX. This follows a February announcement to add an additional Artemis mission before crewed lunar landings.

title: Studying Pneumonia in Space for Heart Health on Earth
summary: Expedition 74 astronauts on the ISS are studying how pneumonia-causing bacteria may cause long-term heart damage, using stem cell-derived heart tissues to explore new cardiovascular health and infectious disease treatments.

title: NASA’s Planet-Hunting TESS Reveals Dazzling Night Sky
summary: NASA's Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) has released its most comprehensive image of the night sky, showcasing its planet-hunting capabilities. The survey provides a detailed view of stars and celestial objects across the southern sky.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones during this period.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 resupply mission to ISS scrubbed due to weather
summary: SpaceX's CRS-34 resupply mission to the International Space Station (ISS) was scrubbed due to weather conditions. The delay comes shortly after the Progress MS-34 cargo spacecraft docked with the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are set to conduct nine launches in the week of May 11. The busy launch manifest highlights increased activity in both the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19 successfully performed a 33-engine static fire test on May 7, 2026. The milestone marks progress toward an upcoming launch.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making progress toward reusability in spaceflight.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion after the end of Flight 11, with Flight 12 nearing completion. The old design is being upgraded for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before an upcoming surge in activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying the CAS500-2 satellite and 45 other payloads to a sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight with a revised trajectory. The return of Starship launches is expected within weeks.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice in the provided news snippet, indicating a potential focus on business-to-business interactions in the space industry.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and validating materials for the European Space Agency's missions to ensure they withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance accessibility and innovation in the space sector.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test, likely related to a landing system for a spacecraft or rover. The term 'bake-out' suggests a thermal testing process to ensure the parachute's integrity under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology is being developed to improve the handling of space debris and satellite servicing. This innovation could significantly enhance the safety and efficiency of space operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026. The event includes an online preparatory forum and an in-person forum at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for the missile-defense program, stating it does not accurately reflect what they are building. The criticism highlights a significant discrepancy in cost projections.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company, along with mentorship, defense market assistance, and business development support.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined the preliminary plan for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch to test critical capabilities for a planned Moon landing in 2028. The mission will involve astronauts traveling to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) as part of the preparations.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking a significant milestone for the company. This event introduces a new player in the space launch industry.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the cost of the Golden Dome program at $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by Gen. Michael Guetlein in March.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to send an automated mini lab to the International Space Station (ISS). The Orbital High-Throughput Screening Device is designed to fit into standard mid-deck lockers aboard crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff but left after Administrator Jared Isaacman took over. His new role has been described as turning political.

title: Star Catcher Closes $65M Series A
summary: Star Catcher has secured $65 million in a Series A funding round led by B Capital, with Shield Capital and Cerberus Ventures co-leading the investment.

title: Fenix Space Flies Tow-Launch Prototype
summary: Fenix Space has successfully flown a tow-launch prototype, marking a significant milestone in their development. The company is advancing toward its goal of commercial spaceflight.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands, Pivots Business—and Raises $275M
summary: Aetherflux has rebranded to Cowboy Space Corporation and raised $275 million to align with its new business direction.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three major U.S. mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—have formed a joint venture to collaborate on direct-to-device (D2D) services, aiming to enhance competition in this space.

title: Intuitive Machines Boosts Deep Space Communications With Goonhilly Acquisition
summary: Intuitive Machines has acquired the U.K.-based Goonhilly Earth Station to expand its deep space communication capabilities. The acquisition strengthens Intuitive Machines' ground station network for supporting lunar and deep space missions.

title: Iridium to Fully Acquire Aireon, Points to Space-Based VHF Opportunity
summary: Iridium is fully acquiring its partner Aireon, which operates a space-based air traffic surveillance system using payloads on Iridium’s network. The acquisition highlights a growing opportunity in space-based VHF (Very High Frequency) applications.

title: Space42 Reports Space Services Revenue Growth Despite Flat Results
summary: Space42 reported flat overall revenues but strong growth in space services revenue for the quarter ending March. The company's performance highlights a shift in revenue streams within the space industry.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch's low-cost Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this effort.

title: Neo Space Group Selects ThinKom Antenna for Narrow-Body Installations
summary: Neo Space Group has chosen ThinKom's ThinAir Ka2517 phased array antenna for narrow-body aircraft installations across multiple airlines. ThinKom announced this adoption, highlighting its multi-orbit capabilities.

title: Eutelsat Shows Strong Growth in LEO Services and Mobility in Q3
summary: Eutelsat reported strong growth in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) services and mobility for Q3, with LEO-driven revenue increasing by 65% year-over-year. Overall revenue reached 293 million euros ($343 million).

title: Bringing LEO to Private Networks: Logos Space Engineering VP Explains the Constellation’s Enterprise-Focused Design
summary: Logos Space is developing a satellite constellation focused on private, secure networks for enterprises and governments. The company has secured FCC approval to proceed with its plans.

title: Networks Business Propels Revenue Growth for SES, Operator Cancels 2 GEO Sats
summary: SES reports revenue growth driven by its Networks business, despite canceling two geostationary (GEO) satellites. The company's financial results align with analyst expectations.

title: AST SpaceMobile Confirms Target for 45 BlueBirds This Year, Despite Blue Origin Launch Failure
summary: AST SpaceMobile aims to deploy 45 BlueBird satellites in 2024, despite a recent Blue Origin launch failure. CEO Abel Avellan reaffirmed the target during an investor update.
